audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Sarah's death, Abraham as sojourner, Hittite negotiation, burial possession, Ephron/Machpelah purchase language, and legal transfer formula. Final review may refine ancient transaction idioms.

verses.restored: The life of Sarah was one hundred twenty-seven years; these were the years of Sarah’s life.

familiar.text: And Sarah was an hundred and seven and twenty years old...

notes.title: Sarah’s life
notes.body: Sarah is the only matriarch whose age at death is recorded in Genesis.

verses.restored: Sarah died in Kiriath-arba, that is Hebron, in the land of Canaan. Abraham came to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.

familiar.text: And Sarah died in Kirjatharba; the same is Hebron...

verses.restored: Abraham rose from before his dead and spoke to the sons of Heth, saying,

familiar.text: And Abraham stood up from before his dead, and spake unto the sons of Heth...

verses.restored: I am a sojourner and resident foreigner among you. Give me a burial possession among you, so that I may bury my dead from before me.

familiar.text: I am a stranger and a sojourner with you: give me a possession of a buryingplace...

notes.title: Sojourner and resident foreigner
notes.body: Ger vetoshav names Abraham’s status: present in the land but not yet its owner.

verses.restored: The sons of Heth answered Abraham, saying to him,

familiar.text: And the children of Heth answered Abraham, saying unto him,

verses.restored: Hear us, my lord. You are a prince of the Divine among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our burial places; none of us will withhold his burial place from you, from burying your dead.

familiar.text: Hear us, my lord: thou art a mighty prince among us...

notes.title: Prince of the Divine
notes.body: Nesi Elohim can mean mighty prince or prince of God/the Divine. The phrase marks Abraham’s honored status among the Hittites.

verses.restored: Abraham rose and bowed to the people of the land, to the sons of Heth.

familiar.text: And Abraham stood up, and bowed himself to the people of the land...

verses.restored: He spoke with them, saying, If it is your soul’s will that I bury my dead from before me, hear me and intercede for me with Ephron son of Zohar,

familiar.text: And he communed with them, saying, If it be your mind that I should bury my dead...

verses.restored: so that he may give me the cave of Machpelah, which belongs to him, at the end of his field. For full silver let him give it to me among you as a burial possession.

familiar.text: That he may give me the cave of Machpelah, which he hath...

notes.title: Full silver
notes.body: Abraham seeks a paid, legally recognized possession, not a temporary favor.

verses.restored: Now Ephron was sitting among the sons of Heth. Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham in the ears of the sons of Heth, before all who entered the gate of his city, saying,

familiar.text: And Ephron dwelt among the children of Heth: and Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham...

notes.title: At the gate
notes.body: The transaction occurs publicly at the city gate, the legal/social setting.

verses.restored: No, my lord, hear me. I give you the field, and the cave that is in it I give to you. Before the eyes of the sons of my people I give it to you. Bury your dead.

familiar.text: Nay, my lord, hear me: the field give I thee, and the cave...

notes.title: Gift language in negotiation
notes.body: Ephron offers gift language, but Abraham insists on payment, likely to secure uncontested ownership.

verses.restored: Abraham bowed before the people of the land.

familiar.text: And Abraham bowed down himself before the people of the land.

verses.restored: He spoke to Ephron in the ears of the people of the land, saying, If only you would hear me. I give the silver for the field; take it from me, and I will bury my dead there.

familiar.text: And he spake unto Ephron in the audience of the people...

verses.restored: Ephron answered Abraham, saying to him,

familiar.text: And Ephron answered Abraham, saying unto him,

verses.restored: My lord, hear me. Land worth four hundred shekels of silver—what is that between me and you? Bury your dead.

familiar.text: My lord, hearken unto me: the land is worth four hundred shekels of silver...

notes.title: Four hundred shekels
notes.body: Ephron names the price in the form of polite negotiation.

verses.restored: Abraham listened to Ephron, and Abraham weighed out for Ephron the silver that he had spoken in the ears of the sons of Heth: four hundred shekels of silver, passing to the merchant.

familiar.text: And Abraham hearkened unto Ephron; and Abraham weighed to Ephron the silver...

verses.restored: So Ephron’s field, which was in Machpelah before Mamre—the field and the cave that was in it, and every tree that was in the field, within all its surrounding boundary—was confirmed

familiar.text: And the field of Ephron, which was in Machpelah... were made sure

notes.title: Confirmed
notes.body: The legal transfer formula begins here and continues through the next verse.

verses.restored: to Abraham as a purchased possession before the eyes of the sons of Heth, among all who entered the gate of his city.

familiar.text: Unto Abraham for a possession in the presence of the children of Heth...

verses.restored: After this, Abraham buried Sarah his woman in the cave of the field of Machpelah before Mamre, that is Hebron, in the land of Canaan.

familiar.text: And after this, Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Machpelah...

verses.restored: The field and the cave that is in it were confirmed to Abraham as a burial possession from the sons of Heth.

familiar.text: And the field, and the cave that is therein, were made sure unto Abraham...

notes.title: First secured landholding
notes.body: The first legally secured piece of promised land is a burial place for Sarah.
